methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Evolv Technology operates in the Defense & Security sector as a public company traded on NASDAQ under ticker EVLV, having raised over $400M in cumulative funding. The comparable universe for this stage and sector consists of 2 public peers and 8 private companies, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI, though this remains a fragmented set without sufficient clustering by stage or valuation tier.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable in this instance. The Defense & Security sector contains 21 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than three datapoints in any category—below the minimum threshold of three required for a defensible median. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity in business models, maturity, and capital intensity across Defense & Security subsegments.

Without sufficient narrow-set peers to triangulate, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range or confidence interval based on the data provided. Resolution would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent defense verticals with methodological documentation of the stretch, or (2) application of alternative methods such as DCF or precedent transaction analysis, for which inputs were not supplied. The key uncertainty remains the absence of directly comparable public or private companies at Evolv's stage and within its narrower subsector, limiting quantitative benchmarking rigor.
